Prison rules are strict. 28, there is no talking with other inmates. No mobile phones or clocks. Clients get a blue prison uniform, a yoga mat, a tea set, a pen and a notebook. They sleep on the floor. There is a small toilet inside the room, but no mirror. The co-founder said the mock prison was 29 by her husband, a lawyer who often put in 100-hour work weeks. “He would rather go into solitary prison for a week to take a rest 30 work for such a long time,” she said. “After a stay in the prison, this is not a prison; the real prison is the place we go back,” many people said. In the highly competitive society in South Korea, there is no surprising that people regard locking themselves up for 24 hours or 48 hours as a way to 31 the stress of life.

questions.subject: 英文
questions.unit: 107-5-四技二專-00共同科目 英文
questions.q: Which of the following countries may Christy live in?
options: New Zealand. ▲ 閱讀下文,回答第 34-37 題。 November 25th is the World Day against Foie Gras. Many people try to express opposition to the torture of force-feeding ducks and geese. Force-feeding causes severe pain and suffering. Over 30 million male ducks undergo this torture every year, in France alone. The fate of females is awful, too: part of them are killed shortly after hatching because they are just not profitable enough. In order to produce Foie Gras, ducks and geese are force-fed with hydraulic pump, several times per day, until their livers grow up to 10 times their normal size. To stop the animals from escaping, they are limited in small cages where they can never stretch their wings. They have no access to swimming or washing water. During the force-feeding period, the mortality rate is up to 20 times more than in traditional bird rearing centers.
questions.explanation: Christy 可能住在下列哪個國家? 垃圾被帶到垃圾變能源的廠房,在那兒,垃圾被載入焚 (A) 泰國。 (B) 墨西哥。 (C) 印度。 (D) 紐西蘭。 化爐燃燒,產生大量的蒸氣轉動發電機的渦輪,再產生 由文章中 Christy 五月穿厚外套,可推知她位於南半 國內電網的電力,在回收上所有剩餘的金屬分開並回 球,因此選 (D) 紐西蘭 收。而其他無法燃燒的材料,如瓷器或磁磚,則收集起 Question 34-37 來當作道路建設的小石礫。在過去十年來,瑞典已經用 11 月 25 日是世界反鵝肝日,很多人試著表達反對強 光境內的垃圾,必須從其他國家,像是英國,進口垃圾, 迫灌食鴨和鵝,強迫灌食會導致嚴重疼痛和苦難,光在 這對多數的國家是個雙贏的局面,因為運送垃圾到瑞典 法國每年超過三千萬的公鴨遭受這樣的折磨,母鴨的命 不僅比掩埋繳稅還便宜,而且可讓更多垃圾加以重新利 運也很悲慘:部分的母鴨在孵化後,旋即被撲殺只因利 用,在這一段時間瑞典已經變成了零垃圾的國家且鼓勵 潤有限。 其他國家考慮此方案。 為了生產鵝肝醬,鴨和鵝一天被液壓幫浦多次強迫

There is a conflict between making money and treating animals in a humane way. ▲ 閱讀下文,回答第 38-41 題。 An incredibly huge amount of trash is dumped into landfills every year around the globe. If you lined it up in garbage trucks, it could circle the Earth 24 times. Sweden has found a way to turn their trash into gold. About half or 47% of Sweden’s waste is recycled. The program recycles and sorts metals, plastics, newspapers, batteries, and electronics. The remaining 52% of the country’s waste like excluded scrap, circuit boards, and agricultural waste is used for the country’s waste-to-energy program, or WTE program, which produces electricity from burning waste. So what is left is one remarkable percent of Sweden’s trash that actually ends up in a landfill. But how exactly does garbage turn into energy? After recyclable content has been removed, the trash is brought to the waste-to-energy plant. There, the trash gets loaded into furnaces and burned, which generates a large amount of steam that spins generator turbines, producing electricity for the nation’s power grid. On recycling, all leftover metals are separated and recycled while the other remaining materials that cannot be burned, like porcelain or tile, are collected for gravel in road construction. Over the past decade, Sweden has run out of their own garbage and had to start importing trash from other countries like UK. It’s a win-win situation for most countries since shipping trash to Sweden is not only cheaper than landfill taxes, but it also makes sure more garbage is reused. Over time, Sweden has become a zero waste nation and encouraged other countries to consider it.
